chunks.text: CS Major Exceptions/ Waiver Requests and Appeals Process - EECS at Berkeley CS Major Exceptions/ Waiver Requests and Appeals Process Exceptions and Waivers to CS Major Requirements Requests for exceptions and waivers to the CS Major Requirements are allowable under the discretion of a committee composed of the CS Vice Chair and Director of CS Student Affairs and will be approved on a case-by-case basis. What is an Exception or Waiver? Students may submit a request for an exception if it is believed that a requirement for the Computer Science major has been fulfilled through the completion of another course, some other experience, program, or test score. The grade and units for that course will be used toward fulfillment of degree requirements. How to Request an Exception or Waiver to CS Major Requirements All requests for exception or waiver to the Computer Science major requirements must be submitted online via the CS Exceptions and Waiver to Major Requirements Form. Computer Science Major Academic Progress Report (APR) Corrections Form Use this form to make a correction on your APR for your Computer Science major . Please allow up to two weeks for processing. If your APR has not been updated after two weeks, email cs-advising@cs.berkeley.edu . Request to Increase Semester Units If you would like to request an increase in the number of units you have for a semester, please submit the Request to Increase Units form. Note: Units will be increased at the discretion of the CS Advisors. We will

two weeks, email cs-advising@cs.berkeley.edu . Request to Increase Semester Units If you would like to request an increase in the number of units you have for a semester, please submit the Request to Increase Units form. Note: Units will be increased at the discretion of the CS Advisors. We will begin to review and notify you during the 2nd week of the semester. Appeal to the CS Major Only students who were admitted prior to 2023 are eligible to submit a CS major appeal. Students who demonstrate extraordinary circumstances such as those involving health or mental health, injury, work obligations, family crisis, etc. are eligible to submit an appeal to the CS major application decision should they not be admitted. To submit an appeal to the CS major, students will be required to write a personal statement and provide supporting documentation . Please thoroughly review the following information about submitting an appeal to the CS Major Application Decision. About the Personal Statement The CS Major Appeal must include a personal statement that is no more than 3000 characters (or 1 page). The statement should include the following information: Note how your academic performance has been affected by extenuating circumstances involving health or mental health, injury, work obligations, family crisis, etc. Describe the challenges in your academic performance and how you are actively overcoming them. Detail the activities and experiences you have/will use to supplement your learning such as an internship, research, CS-related employment, tutoring, mentoring, on-campus involvement/leadership, etc. Supporting Documentation

health or mental health, injury, work obligations, family crisis, etc. Describe the challenges in your academic performance and how you are actively overcoming them. Detail the activities and experiences you have/will use to supplement your learning such as an internship, research, CS-related employment, tutoring, mentoring, on-campus involvement/leadership, etc. Supporting Documentation Students must submit at least one strong letter of recommendation for their appeal. Letters of recommendation may come from: Supervisors from a CS-related internship Supervisors/faculty mentors of a CS-related research center/research lab position or Academic Intern/UGSI position (from Berkeley or another institution including community college) Only submit letters from professors that supervised your work on non-classroom related projects or research. Letters should NOT come from: Professors whose class you took and with whom you have no outside classroom relationship TA’s Timeline CS Major Appeals are reviewed by the CS Vice Chair and Director of CS Student Affairs throughout the semester. Students will be notified via email once a decision is made. Work with a CDSS advisor to determine your deadline to declare CS. Steps to Submitting an Appeal You can submit an appeal after all the grades have been posted and you have been denied after submitting a CS major application. Gather all documents needed including a personal statement and at least one letter of recommendation. All appeals must be submitted online using the CS Major Appeals Form.
